site.name: Mr.Lee's Lair
site.description: Portfolio of Lee Gwangyeol — frontend web developer and factory automation engineer. Experience in Next.js, TypeScript, React, C#, .NET-based web and industrial automation systems.
site.h1: Lee Gwangyeol — Frontend Web Developer · Automation Robotics Engineer Portfolio

hero.name: Lee Gwangyeol
hero.role: Bridging the web and robotic automation
hero.summary:
💻 A frontend web developer and 🤖 automation robotics engineer.
I build web products with Next.js and factory-automation systems with C#.

I'm deeply interested in the convergence of AI and robotic automation,
and pursue technical innovation through 📚 continuous learning and 🚀 bold challenges.

wintech2025.description: Improved equipment UI/UX (refactoring), developed special handlers for overseas clients.

poul.description: Frontend development for the llami project. Built a modular Next.js + TypeScript frontend architecture, applied optimistic fetch, managed the Git workflow, and set up a UI component test environment.

wintech2021.description: Led production-automation equipment projects. Developed PC-based automation systems on C#/.NET, ran a press-handler integration project, and built MES integrations and hardware communication units.

enitec.description: Analyzed and tested semiconductor equipment logs, designed embedded systems, and developed WinActor libraries (Excel automation, AI-OCR).

hufs.degree: B.A. in History (Double major in Russian)

ham.name: HAM Series
ham.description: Code refactoring, WinForms → WPF migration (MVVM pattern), front/back structuring, and introduction of equipment setup automation.

llami.name: llami Project
llami.description: Frontend development of an AI chatbot. Built a SOLID-principle-based modular architecture, implemented consultation-request and customer-contact management, and shipped a voice-input UX.

press.name: Press Handler Integration
press.description: Factory-automation equipment development. Multi-threaded axis control, safe motion-sequence communication, and inter-equipment hardware communication.

introduce.metaDescription: Values and core competencies of Lee Gwangyeol — challenge mindset, conviction in future tech, growth through overcoming gaps, hardware-software understanding, code-quality awareness, and turning dreams into reality.
introduce.h1: About Lee Gwangyeol — Values and Core Competencies
introduce.title: Values and Core Competencies
introduce.subtitle: Six short essays on what I think and what I've lived through.
introduce.ogAlt: About Lee Gwangyeol — Values and Core Competencies
introduce.ogSubtitle: Six essays on what I think and what I've lived

challenge.content: One of the most important values in my life is the belief that "nothing is impossible." In college, I started Russian later than most, but once I committed I followed through — intensive self-study via YouTube, language exchanges with locals while studying in Russia, and ultimately a language certification. Driven by the goal of working abroad as an engineer, I went through roughly a year of developer training and then built a career as a developer in Japan. From there I moved into robotic automation, working on-site in Japan and China, and most recently expanded into web development and AI. 💪 🎯 ⚔️

future.content: In the early 2010s, the consensus was that cryptocurrency was worthless because it had "no substance" — a call that aged badly given today's US–China race for crypto dominance. AI's path is similar: rather than being a distant promise, LLM- and RAG-armed systems are already deployed across many domains. The humanoid robots being built by OpenAI and China's Unitree make the trajectory clear — the future will be reorganized around AI-driven robotics. I'm investing in that conviction now: continuously learning and pushing to be ready for the era of commercialized AI robotics. I've been burned before by failing to prepare for futures I already saw coming. I won't repeat it. 🤖 💻 🧑‍💻

overcome.content: Through years of automation-equipment work, I found real satisfaction in identifying on-site needs and turning them into working systems. Communicating directly with engineers and clients in Japan and China let me use my language skills, and I enjoyed going on-site domestically and abroad. Feedback like "production runs smoothly now" and "this is much better than the previous machine" built real confidence. But I also kept hitting my own limits — design sense, UX, API design, server-side understanding, and the UI ceiling of C#-only stacks. To close those gaps I studied on my own, and through a referral joined a chatbot-development web startup to gain hands-on experience. 🧗🏻 🛠️ 🚀

hardware.content: Through pandemic-era trips to sites in Japan and China, I often had to make hardware modifications myself due to staffing shortages. Adjusting path-lines during equipment setup, measuring vibration and resonance from multi-axis motion, and proposing fixes taught me firsthand how software affects physical robots. Differences in lighting between the in-house setup and the customer site, lens damage during shipping, and similar details — none of which are visible from the code alone — gave me an instinct for what to anticipate as a robotics developer, which I now apply to production-sequence efficiency (vibration removal) and UX design. 🖥️ 🖨️ ⌨️

quality.categoryValue: A shift in how I see code quality
quality.content: When I first worked on automation equipment, I was afraid to change existing code — the swarm of compiler errors and unpredictable side effects made me conservative, with a "if it runs, it's fine" mindset. Exposure to SOLID-driven development during chatbot work changed that. I came to appreciate code clarity and structure, and the value of writing code that is maintainable and extensible — not just code that happens to work. That shift fundamentally changed my engineering philosophy, and continues to drive my pursuit of better code. 🎯 🏆 ⚙️

dream.content: I don't let dreams stay as imagination — I turn them into reality. The college dream of learning Russian became actual study in Russia and a language certification. The dream of working abroad became real careers in Japan and China. Along the way, I learned the importance of breaking a dream into concrete steps and taking action on each one. My current dream is to use AI and robotics to build services that create social value — and I'm steadily preparing by learning relevant tech, joining diverse projects, and building toward an actual product. ✨ 🌛 💫
